attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker who exploits tactical disorganization in breakaways rather than raw power. Responds early on climbs but struggles to sustain efforts to the finish.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support, often functioning as first responder domestique for DSM. Required to make solo bridging efforts without dedicated lead-out or positioning help.
terrain_preference: Breakaway scenarios on mountainous stages where tactical chaos creates opportunities. Exposed on sustained climbs requiring deep reserves and in organized bunch sprints.
vulnerability: Lacks finishing reserves after initial climb responses or bridging efforts. Energy management issues when required to chase back after setbacks. Cannot compete when outnumbered without tactical leverage.
key_patterns: First to respond on climbs but fades before finish (2 observations)
Requires massive solo efforts to establish position, compromising later performance (2 observations)
Succeeds by exploiting opponents' tactical mistakes rather than superior strength (2 observations)
Functions in domestique role despite breakaway ambitions, splitting effort allocation (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Recent Vuelta appearances show declining finishing power and increased reliance on unsustainable bridging efforts
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 9
